title: fix port -l timing with healthchecks
body:
many of the port tests use our nginx container image.  in some cases, we have timing
issues between when the nginx and the container are running and when the port -l
command is run causing test flakes.  we now use the container image's built in
healthcheck to ensure that nginx is running (and subsequently the container
itself) before running the port command.

Fixes: #3309

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

taking a stab at this from a mac, so not fully vetted.  @edsantiago @mheon ptal
One nit, otherwise it looks fine.

LGTM 
Other then fixing the import ordering.

> Minor nits, plus one question: how in heck does this work? When I try to test it on my end, I get:
> 
> ```
> # podman container run -dt --name foo -P quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest;podman healthcheck run foo
> 34fd2224ed124b9105e1ca6cfc4734637d569ae6bc0033513cfc8ee936c133a6
> Error: container 34fd2224ed124b9105e1ca6cfc4734637d569ae6bc0033513cfc8ee936c133a6 has no defined healthcheck
> ```
> 
> What am I missing?

I bet that quay is discarding the healthcheck.  I will have to verify that first.
But if it's quay, how can CI be passing?
